remarks.name: Tigera Blog (Calico / AI Security)
remarks.role: AI agent governance, security, accountability frameworks, enterprise AI deployment policy.
remarks.text: Why We Built Lynx: Bringing Control to the Age of AI Agents
remarks.content: For a decade, one idea has guided everything we’ve built at Tigera: How do you secure a dynamic system with a lot of moving parts that is changing rapidly, with a programmatic approach? Calico has... The post Why We Built Lynx: Bringing Control to the Age of AI Agents appeared first on Tigera
remarks.contentTranslation: 十年来，一个想法指导了我们在Tigera构建的一切：如何通过编程方法来确保一个具有许多快速变化的移动部件的动态系统？ Calico有...我们为什么要建造Lynx ：将控制权带入AI时代的文章首次出现在Tigera上
remarks.translation: 我们为什么要构建Lynx ：将控制权带入AI智能体时代
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Governance as acceleration: data proves it’s not a speed bump
remarks.content: For years, the enterprise AI governance conversation has been shaped by a quiet assumption: Governance is the thing that slows AI down. This may look like the review cycle that delays the launch, committee that questions the model, or even documentation requirement that sits between a wo
remarks.contentTranslation: 多年来，企业AI治理对话一直被一个安静的假设所塑造：治理是减缓AI的因素。这可能看起来像延迟发布的审核周期、质疑模型的委员会，甚至是WO之间的文档要求
remarks.translation: 治理作为加速：数据证明它不是减速带
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Generative AI governance framework: building responsible AI systems
remarks.content: Generative AI is no longer a pilot that leaders can observe from a distance. It is in contact centers, developer workflows, reporting layers, and autonomous agents acting on behalf of the business. As BARC's research on modernizing governance for the era of agentic AI shows, the stakes r
remarks.contentTranslation: 生成式AI不再是领导者可以远距离观察的试点。它存在于联系中心、开发人员工作流、报告层和代表业务行事的自主Agent中。正如BARC关于智能AI时代治理现代化的研究所表明的那样，
remarks.translation: 生成式AI治理框架：构建负责任的AI系统

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Sovereignty isn't where your AI runs. It's whether it answers to you.
remarks.content: Last week, a government order took the world's best AI model offline in hours. No one deleted it and no server failed. A single directive put the most capable model on the market out of reach for everyone not a citizen of one country.
remarks.contentTranslation: 上周，一项政府命令在数小时内让世界上最好的AI模型下线。没有人将其删除，也没有服务器发生故障。单一指令使市场上最有能力的模型对每个人不是一个国家的公民来说都是遥不可及的。
remarks.translation: 主权并不是你的AI运行的地方。问题在于它是否对你有所回应。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: What is an AI orchestration layer? Architecture, benefits, and enterprise use cases
remarks.content: AI investments are multiplying, but coordination between models, agents, and data pipelines remains an afterthought in most enterprises. The result is predictable: duplicated workflows, inconsistent outputs, ungoverned decisions, and AI initiatives that never graduate from pilot to produ
remarks.contentTranslation: AI投资正在成倍增加，但在大多数企业中，模型、Agent和数据管道之间的协调仍然是事后的想法。结果是可预测的：重复的工作流、不一致的输出、不受控制的决策以及从试点到生产的AI计划
remarks.translation: 什么是AI编排层？架构、优势和企业用例
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: AI explainability in enterprise AI: methods, tools, and why it matters for trustworthy models and agents
remarks.content: AI models, generative AI (GenAI) applications, and agents are making decisions that affect credit scores, patient care, and hiring outcomes. Enterprises deploying these systems are accumulating regulatory and reputational risk proportional to the number of decisions that cannot be explai
remarks.contentTranslation: AI模型、生成式AI(GenAI)应用程序和Agent商正在做出影响信用评分、患者护理和招聘结果的决策。部署这些系统的企业正在积累与无法解释的决策数量成比例的监管和声誉风险
remarks.translation: 企业AI中的AI可解释性：方法、工具及其对可信模型和Agent的重要性
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Agentic AI tools in 2026: what to look for when choosing an enterprise-grade solution
remarks.content: Most vendors claim their agentic AI platform is enterprise-grade. In a market where the term has lost almost all meaning, the real test is not the demo. It is what happens after, when agents meet real data, real governance requirements, and the organizational complexity that no sandbox c
remarks.contentTranslation: 大多数供应商声称他们的AgentAI平台是企业级的。在这个术语几乎失去所有意义的市场中，真正的考验不是演示。当座席满足真实数据、真实治理要求以及没有沙箱的组织复杂性时，
remarks.translation: 2026年的Agentic AI工具：在选择企业级解决方案时要注意什么

remarks.name: Amjad Masad
remarks.role: ceo replit. civilizationist
remarks.text: We posted for twenty years, thinking we were talking to each other. Then the transformer came online, and the network read what we’d written, and became itself.
remarks.translation: 我们发了二十年的帖子，以为我们在互相交谈。然后变压器上线了，网络读了我们写的东西，变成了它自己。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Zara Zhang
remarks.role: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w
remarks.text:
I hoard X bookmarks and never read them. So I built an extension that injects a bookmarked post into my main feed every time I open X (almost like an ad). Now I read my bookmarks. 

The trick was hijacking real estate I already check 50 times a day https://t.co/pmrZGNaji5
remarks.translation: 我囤积了X个书签，从不阅读。因此，我构建了一个扩展程序，每次打开X （几乎就像广告一样）时，都会将书签帖子注入到我的主要Feed中。现在我读了我的书签。诀窍是劫持房地产，我已经每天检查50次
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Thibault Sottiaux
remarks.role: Codex & ChatGPT OpenAI
remarks.text:
We built the Codex App with models that were okayish at front-end.

Wait to see what we can do when we finally improve front-end capabilities significantly in our models. That day will be something.
remarks.translation: 我们用前端还不错的模型构建了Codex应用程序。等着瞧，当我们最终在我们的模型中显着提高前端功能时，我们能做些什么。那一天将是有意义的。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
Pretty remarkable what’s happening with open weights AI right now. We’re seeing models achieve SOTA results on specific tasks, and getting close to frontier on some areas of coding and other domains. 

The more that open weights is able to maintain only a marginal gap from the frontier, instead of a widening gap, the more value that can be created with AI.

Incidentally, this is actually fine for the frontier labs as well; if we can lower the cost of an overall task then AI usage goes up in general. You’re still likely using frontier models for planning, orchestration, reviewing, and other parts of work. 

But this is all very good for the applied layer of AI, which is now in a great position to cost optimize workloads with cheaper models or use tailored open models post-trained for specific tasks to improve performance.
remarks.translation:
现在开放式权重AI正在发生的事情非常了不起。我们看到模型在特定任务上实现了SOTA结果，并在编码和其他领域的某些领域接近前沿。开放的权重越能够保持与前沿的边际差距，而不是扩大差距，AI可以创造的价值就越多。
顺便说一句，这实际上对前沿实验室来说也很好；如果我们能够降低整体任务的成本，那么AI的使用量就会普遍增加。您仍有可能使用前沿模型来规划、编排、审核和其他工作。但这对于AI的应用层来说是非常好的，现在AI可以使用更便宜的模型来优化工作负载，或者使用针对特定任务进行后期训练的定制开放模型来提高性能。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.text:
I will go against the grain and say I can barely use up my Codex and Claude $200 subscriptions so I don’t see the point of trying local models…

Also to try the latest glm locally requires 512 mb which is like a $10K Max Studio?

remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Nikunj Kothari
remarks.role: partner fpvventures - investing in seed/A. previous: early hire meter, opendoor, atlassian & others. love shimoleejhaveri + 👦👧
remarks.text:
The biggest problem with AI is that priors need to be reset every few weeks.. and it seems like most people are incapable of doing that. 

I talk to so many people who say xyz doesn’t work and when I ask when was the last time they tried testing it, the answer is always “a few months ago.” 

Brother that’s like eons ago in AI timelines. This is why each person needs to have their own evals of hard tasks and weekly tinker time to truly understand where the frontier is. 

And then you need to talk to enterprise buyers weekly who are usually two years behind. But they are the buyers so you need to understand how to market. 

The combination of the two will give you a pretty good sense of where to invest time and capital. If you just do these two weekly, you’ll be ahead of 99% of people.
remarks.translation:
AI最大的问题是，先验需要每隔几周重置一次……似乎大多数人都无法做到这一点。我和很多说xyz不起作用的人交谈，当我问他们上次尝试测试它是什么时候时，答案总是“几个月前”。“哥哥，在AI时间表中，那是很久以前的事了。这就是为什么每个人都需要有自己的艰巨任务和每周的修补时间来真正了解边界在哪里。
然后，您需要每周与通常落后两年的企业买家交谈。但他们是买家，因此您需要了解如何营销。两者的结合将使您很好地了解在哪里投入时间和资金。如果你每周做这两件事，你将领先于99%的人。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Madhu Guru
remarks.role: Prev: Product Leader at Google - Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
remarks.text:
The Product role is having an identity crisis too.

Engineering has found its AI-native interface - SWE agents dramatically increase individual leverage.
Companies are asking PMs to use AI, but they haven't evolved the role.

So there are two camps.

The old-school PM.
AI accelerates the old job: more PRDs, more strategy decks, more docs. Lots of output, not much judgment.

The Builder PM.
Builder PMs use AI to expand their role across the product lifecycle.

They explore a much larger surface area of ideas to arrive at the best. They run agents for market and user research, query logs and analytics directly, generate competing ideas, then curate the best ones.

They constantly evolve their workflows to take advantage of the latest in AI.

Their outputs are increasingly prototypes rather than docs - engineers react far more constructively to demos than docs.

They do this all without compromising on the craft -they still form a strong point of view on what should be built and why.

I think the role is moving much closer to Builder PMs.
remarks.translation:
产品角色也面临身份危机。工程部门发现了其AI原生界面--SWEAgent大大提高了个人杠杆率。公司要求项目经理使用AI，但他们还没有改变这一角色。所以有两个阵营。老派的PM。AI加速了旧的工作：更多的PRD ，更多的战略卡组，更多的文档。很多产出，没有太多判断力。Builder PM。Builder PM使用AI在整个产品生命周期中扩展其角色。
他们探索更大的创意表面积，以达到最佳效果。他们直接运行Agent进行市场和用户研究，查询日志和分析，生成相互竞争的创意，然后策划最好的创意。他们不断改进工作流，以利用最新的AI技术。他们的输出越来越多地是原型而不是文档-工程师对演示的反应比文档更具建设性。
他们这样做并不会影响工艺-他们仍然对应该建造什么以及为什么建造形成了强有力的观点。我认为这个角色越来越接近Builder项目经理。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。
